objective: Repair `imobench/answerbench_v2.csv` so `imo-bench-algebra-036` has the reported short answer `$Y(x)=A+B/x-x$` in its own Short Answer column and again has exactly the six columns declared by the dataset header.
completion_criteria: Parse the CSV with a standards-compliant reader, assert that every row has six columns, assert the exact short-answer field for `imo-bench-algebra-036`, and pass any repository checks. The current main branch was independently reproduced as a five-column row.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: Run 3 refreshed the official source state, replayed the complete candidate gate and a materially separate checker, and added a checked one-byte patch artifact. The candidate exactly repairs algebra-036 and satisfies the full local verification contract. No external action was taken.

established_facts.claim: The pinned upstream CSV has exactly 400 data records under ordinary parsing, with width counts {5: 1, 6: 399}; the width-five record is algebra-036.
A quote inserted at byte offset 13,618 restores six fields on every record and exact target Short Answer $Y(x)=A+\frac{B}{x}-x$.

strategies.mechanism: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.
strategies.hypothesis: A generic guard fails uniquely on the pinned malformed file and passes after repair.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run it on the pinned and repaired trees; require one failure before and zero after.
strategies.rationale: Potential preventive value, but only worth the scope expansion if maintainers request it.
strategies.title: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.

strategies.title: Replay the hash-pinned one-byte repair through a standards-library CSV harness, adversarial controls, a materially separate exact-diff checker, and a directly applicable patch check after refreshing upstream status.
strategies.mechanism: The issue-supplied answer token fixes the malformed boundary; a strict CSV harness reconstructs and attacks the candidate, while a separate checker enforces exact schema, target fields, and baseline-plus-one-byte equality.
strategies.hypothesis: Against unchanged upstream commit 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace, the materialized candidate is exactly the required one-byte quote insertion, strict-parses as 400 six-field data rows, restores the exact algebra-036 Short Answer, and rejects wrong-answer and collateral-edit controls.
strategies.discriminating_test: Refresh upstream main, issue #13, and target-specific PR searches; if unchanged, run the recorded full harness and standalone checker against the pinned baseline and require all predeclared positive and negative signals.
strategies.blocker: No technical blocker remains. External submission requires human-owner authorization, Google CLA readiness, and maintainer review.
strategies.reopen_condition: Reopen technical work only if upstream main, issue status, competing PR status, target bytes, or maintainer-requested scope changes.
strategies.reopen_evidence: Run-3 live checks showed no upstream movement or competing PR and both technical gates reproduced successfully.

failure_signatures.signature: An unclosed quoted Problem field can be tolerated as a five-field record by ordinary parsing but rejected as malformed by strict parsing; semantic-only repair checks can miss unrelated edits.
Semantic-only validation accepts a repaired file containing an unrelated equal-length problem-ID mutation; exact reference comparison is mandatory.
Semantic-only validation accepts the tested unrelated equal-length edit, so exact baseline comparison is mandatory.

decision_history.prediction: Ordinary parsing would show 399 six-field and one five-field row; a one-byte candidate would yield 400 six-field rows and pass all controls.
decision_history.observation: The width prediction and all candidate controls passed; strict parsing of the malformed baseline raised a CSV syntax error while strict parsing of the repair passed.
decision_history.surprise: The initial strict-parser run failed earlier than the predicted width count, providing a stronger syntax-level diagnostic; the harness was revised to preserve both tolerant-width and strict-syntax signals.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Resolve upstream main; if unchanged, materialize the one-byte candidate and run the narrow checker with the pinned source as --reference.

decision_history.prediction: The unchanged baseline would fail, while the materialized one-byte repair would pass all structural, semantic, and exact-diff gates.
decision_history.observation: All predicted signals occurred; the candidate hash equals the previously derived repair hash, and all negative controls behaved as required.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Immediately before any authorized PR, refresh upstream main, issue state, and competing PRs; rerun the exact-diff checker if unchanged.

decision_history.prediction: The live source gate would remain unchanged and both recorded validations would reproduce all prior PASS/rejection signals.
decision_history.observation: Main remained 96fa6c4, issue #13 remained open, both PR searches returned zero, the full harness returned 0 in 0.588 seconds, and the standalone checker returned 0 in 0.123 seconds.
decision_history.next_discriminator: After explicit owner authorization, repeat the live source gate and standalone checker once; proceed to the minimal PR only if all remain unchanged.
